# PRD: Internationalization (General Translation)

**Issue**: #6
**Status**: Draft (auto-generated by Ink)
**Author**: Ink (auto)

---

## 1. Problem Statement

Blueprint needs AI-powered internationalization so that templates built from it can support multiple languages out of the box. All user-facing strings on landing/public pages and dashboard currently contain hardcoded English text, which prevents non-English users from using the product effectively.

## 2. Goals

- Wrap all user-facing strings with `<T>` translation components
- Set up URL-based locale routing (`/en/`, `/it/`)
- Add a language switcher accessible from public pages (not just dashboard settings)
- Ensure SEO-friendly locale handling with proper `lang` attribute and meta
- Demonstrate the i18n pattern so template users can follow it

## 3. Non-Goals

- Translating Sanity CMS/blog content (Sanity handles that separately)
- RTL language support
- Translation of user-generated content
- Adding more than 2 locales (EN + IT) initially

## 4. Data Model

N/A — No database changes required. General Translation handles translations via its cloud service. Configuration is in `gt.config.json`.

## 5. API / Backend

N/A — No Convex queries/mutations needed. GT uses build-time and runtime translation via the `gt-next` SDK.

## 6. Component Architecture

### New Components
- None required — language switcher already exists at `src/app/dashboard/settings/_components/language-section.tsx`

### Components to Modify (wrap strings with `<T>`)

**Landing pages:**
- `src/app/(landing)/page.tsx` — Hero section (badge, h1, description, buttons, social proof)
- `src/app/(landing)/_components/features-section.tsx` — Section heading + 4 feature cards (title, description)
- `src/app/(landing)/_components/tech-stack-section.tsx` — Section heading + category titles + tech items (name, description, why)
- `src/app/(landing)/_components/faq-section.tsx` — FAQ title, description + 8 Q&A items
- `src/app/(landing)/_components/generic-faq-section.tsx` — "Still have questions?" + "Contact us" text
- `src/app/(landing)/contact/page.tsx` — Page title, description, form labels, buttons, status messages
- `src/app/(landing)/privacy/page.tsx` — Page title (content is Lorem ipsum placeholder, skip)
- `src/app/(landing)/terms/page.tsx` — Page title (content is Lorem ipsum placeholder, skip)

**Layout/Navigation:**
- `src/components/layout/site-header.tsx` — Brand name "Blueprint"
- `src/components/layout/Footer.tsx` — Footer text, link labels, copyright
- `src/components/layout/main-nav.tsx` — Nav items (passed as props from config)
- `src/config/site.ts` — Nav item titles need to be translatable (but these are data, not JSX — needs approach decision)

**Auth pages:**
- `src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx` — Login form labels/text
- `src/app/(auth)/signup/page.tsx` — Signup form labels/text

**Dashboard (partially done):**
- Settings pages already use `<T>` — no changes needed there

### Approach Decision: Data-Driven Strings

For strings in arrays/objects (nav items, features, FAQ, tech stack, footer links), there are two approaches:
1. **Inline `<T>` in JSX** — Move string rendering to JSX and wrap with `<T id="...">`
2. **`useGT()` hook** — Use `t()` function for programmatic translation

Decision: Use `<T id="...">` in JSX wherever possible (GT recommendation). For data arrays, render the `<T>` at the point of use, not in the data definition.

## 7. Security

N/A — No security implications. Translations are public content.

## 8. Performance

- GT translations are fetched at build time and cached
- No runtime API calls for cached translations
- Locale detection adds minimal overhead via middleware
- `<T>` components render synchronously when translations are cached

## 9. i18n

- **Provider**: `gt-next` (already configured)
- **Config**: `gt.config.json` — `defaultLocale: "en"`, `locales: ["en", "it"]`
- **Next.js config**: `withGTConfig()` already wraps `next.config.ts`
- **Root layout**: `<GTProvider>` already wraps the app
- **ID convention**: `<T id="section.key">Default text</T>` — GT uses the default text as source, ID for stable reference
- **URL routing**: GT handles locale routing automatically via middleware integration (needs verification)

## 10. Testing Strategy

- `bun run build` passes with all `<T>` wrappers
- `grep -rn` verification that no hardcoded user-facing strings remain on landing pages
- Language switcher renders and toggles between EN/IT
- URL reflects locale change

## 11. Rollout Plan

1. Wrap all landing page strings with `<T>`
2. Wrap auth page strings
3. Wrap layout/nav strings
4. Add language switcher to public-facing header/footer
5. Verify build passes
6. Test locale switching

## 12. Open Questions

- Does GT handle URL-based locale routing (`/it/`, `/en/`) automatically, or does it need middleware configuration?
- Should the language switcher in the navbar be a dropdown or a separate component?
- Should nav item titles in `config/site.ts` use `<T>` IDs that get resolved at render time?
